page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-12xCPU-32GB is a KVM-hypervised virtual server based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 12 shared vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.67 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is poor, falling into the bottom 10% with a score of 787.29, which is also reflected in a moderate Geekbench single-core score of 1204.0. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 9386.96 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 7735.0. Memory bandwidth is stable, averaging 54.21 GB/sec for large uncached reads and 42.64 GB/sec for large uncached writes.

Given the shared CPU allocation and the lower single-core performance, this server is positioned as a cost-efficient option for workloads that can leverage multi-threaded execution without requiring dedicated CPU resources. It is suitable for multi-threaded decompression tasks, software compilation, and basic web serving, as demonstrated by its average static web serving throughput of 9.33 GB/sec and 17.5 million Redis SET operations per second. It is less suited for single-threaded tasks or intensive real-time LLM text generation, where its 7B model text generation speed drops to a poor 4.94 tokens/sec.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-12xCPU-32GB is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 12 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor at 2.0 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU resources. Benchmark data shows poor single-core performance, placing in the bottom 10% for stress-ng single-core tests, but average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. It achieves a static web serving throughput of 9.33 GB/sec and a multi-core Geekbench score of 7735.0. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and general web serving, though it is less optimal for single-threaded applications or demanding large-scale LLM inference.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-12xCPU-32GB is a KVM-based virtual server with 12 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. It offers average multi-core performance but limited single-core processing power.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-12xCPU-32GB is an x86_64 virtual server with 12 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32.0 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance.
tagline: A shared-resource virtual server offering twelve AMD EPYC cores and thirty-two gigabytes of memory for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 12 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory with 2.67 GB per core
KVM hypervisor architecture with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Average multi-core performance contrasted by poor single-core benchmark results
Well-suited for multi-threaded software compilation and static web serving
